title: Tags, Versions, Releases, and Provenance · Repository Checkpoint
summary: Prepare a release candidate whose source identity, version, evidence, and published reference can be independently verified. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a signed-off release evidence record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish lightweight and annotated tag objects, messages, taggers, targets, and intended use.
Create, inspect, verify, delete, and cautiously replace tags while recognizing immutable-release expectations.
Apply an explicit version scheme and connect version changes to compatibility, release notes, and source identity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Lightweight Annotated Tags · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Release v4.1.0 targets commit a11ce00, but the working tree has an unstaged version edit. Establish source identity, clean state, annotated tag evidence, checksum, and trust limits.

Which action demonstrates Lightweight Annotated Tags without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish lightweight and annotated tag objects, messages, taggers, targets, and intended use.

content.text: Release v4.1.0 targets commit a11ce00, but the working tree has an unstaged version edit. Establish source identity, clean state, annotated tag evidence, checksum, and trust limits.

content.text: Use the supplied trace to distinguish an observed Lightweight Annotated Tags result from an unsupported claim.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Pro Git, Second Edition — Controls cumulative conceptual sequence from setup through internals, collaboration, tooling, and distributed workflows.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Every tag stores a message and tagger identity.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Lightweight Annotated Tags evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Lightweight Annotated Tags assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Release v4.1.0 targets commit a11ce00, but the working tree has an unstaged version edit. Establish source identity, clean state, annotated tag evidence, checksum, and trust limits.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Lightweight Annotated Tags has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Release v5.1.0 targets commit a11ce00, but the working tree has an unstaged version edit. Establish source identity, clean state, annotated tag evidence, checksum, and trust limits.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

stimulus.title: Signature Trust failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A cryptographically valid signature proves the change is safe.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish commit or tag signature validity, signer identity, key trust, account verification, and policy compliance.

options.text: Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Signature Trust: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Release v6.1.0 targets commit a11ce00, but the working tree has an unstaged version edit. Establish source identity, clean state, annotated tag evidence, checksum, and trust limits.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Distinguish commit or tag signature validity, signer identity, key trust, account verification, and policy compliance.
